tagline: Work with Crisp websites, conversations, people, helpdesk, campaigns, plugins, and subscription metadata from the command line.

groups.title: People, Events, Segments, And Campaign Links
Plugins, Connect, Plans, Bucket, And Media

global_flags.name: connection
global_flags.summary: Use a saved Crisp connector credential and website scope.

commands.summary: Creates a new website.

commands.approval: Reverse ETL commands must use plan → preview → approval → execute; no direct write dispatch is exposed by #205.

commands.summary: Resolves messages in an existing conversation. Returns the last batch of messages if there are many messages in the conversation. Then, messages can be paged towards older messages using the timestamp_before parameter, towards more recent messages using the timestamp_after parameter, or centered around a given timestamp using the timestamp_around parameter.

commands.summary: Maps visitors in a geographical area, given a geographical center and a map radius (defaults to whole Earth if none given). Visitors are grouped in geographical points. There is a static number of points per area (the area is composed of sub-divisions of equal size). Only points which contain visitors are returned.

commands.summary: Transforms a token to a session identifier, if any session is bound to the token. The token can be bound to a session from the Crisp chatbox.

help_topics.name: crisp auth
help_topics.summary: Configure Crisp REST API Basic credentials from environment variables or stdin; never pass secret values in prompt text.

help_topics.name: crisp safety
help_topics.summary: Crisp write commands are blocked by default until typed reverse-ETL schemas, risk text, preview, approval, and confirmation gates are implemented.
